description_long: This is a freestanding festival-style awning booth with a rectangular metal frame and a flat, dark canopy roof. The front valance is bright red and prominently displays white Japanese katakana characters, reading 'わなげ', which translates to 'ring toss', suggesting its use as a game stall. The side panels feature alternating vertical red and white stripes, adding a festive and eye-catching appearance. The structure is open at the front, allowing easy access for participants, while the back is closed off with a solid striped panel. The frame appears to be constructed from tubular metal, providing stability and durability for outdoor use. The overall design is typical of temporary booths found at Japanese festivals, fairs, or urban street events.
description: A festive, freestanding Japanese festival booth awning with a red valance displaying 'わなげ' and striped side panels, supported by a sturdy metal frame.
description_view_0: The angled view highlights the red valance with Japanese text and reveals the striped side panel and open front.
description_view_1: The direct front view centers the red valance with 'わなげ' text and shows the open entrance beneath the canopy.
description_view_2: This side-angled view emphasizes the depth of the booth, the flat canopy, and the striped side panel.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the solid striped back panel and the full width of the canopy roof.
attribute_mounting_location: freestanding on flat ground or pavement
attribute_canopy_pattern: solid dark canopy with red and white vertically striped side and back panels
attribute_edge_trim_style: straight edge with a flat valance displaying text
attribute_branding_or_signage: Japanese text 'わなげ' (ring toss) in white on the front red valance
attribute_support_structure_type: fixed frame with four vertical metal posts and horizontal beams
